preview_text:
The model favors Japan at 69%, a commanding margin over Sweden's 7.5% win probability. This 61-percentage-point gap reflects a strong asymmetry in the forecast. Sweden is given a modest but real chance, whereas Japan emerges as a heavy favorite in the model's assessment.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Japan heavily preferred, yet draws are plausible at 23.5%. The model projects this as a relatively one-sided affair in probability space, with Sweden facing a steep climb to victory. A Swedish win remains in the low-single-digit range, indicating the model expects the match to turn on Japan's advantage rather than on genuine competitive balance.

The model and market diverge substantially. The model's 69% for Japan sits 24 percentage points above the sportsbook consensus of 45%, while Sweden's 7.5% sits 19 percentage points below the market's 27%. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; this discrepancy warrants review of the underlying forecast assumptions.
